title: C.G. Jung — Psychological Types (1921) — PRIMARY TEXT DEEP INGEST

concepts.title: Introversion/Extraversion: The Direction of Libido
concepts.description: fundamental attitude determining where psychic energy flows; not social comfort but directional bias; introverts accumulate libido internally (depth); extraverts discharge libido externally (breadth); both necessary; neurotic patterns differ by attitude (introvert withdraws, extravert acts frantically)

concepts.title: Superior Function: The Tyranny and Necessity of the Dominant
concepts.description: the function through which consciousness primarily organizes reality; highly differentiated; reliable but rigid; identification with superior = possession; neurosis through over-extension; compensation generates opposite unconscious content

concepts.title: Compensation Principle: Automatic Psychological Balancing
concepts.description: one-sided consciousness generates equal opposite unconscious content; mechanical, not corrective; the more extreme the conscious position, the more extreme the unconscious opposite; understanding prevents fighting the eruption

concepts.title: Thinking Function: Logic and Discrimination
concepts.description: discriminates true/false; applies logic to determine reality; external (extraverted thinking: logic applied to facts) or internal (introverted thinking: logic applied to principles); superior form brilliant and reliable; inferior form tortured and reactive; primary function in philosophy, science, systems

concepts.title: Feeling Function: Value and Meaning
concepts.description: discriminates valuable/valueless; determines what matters; external (extraverted feeling: values applied to relationships) or internal (introverted feeling: values applied internally as authenticity); superior form creates genuine connection; inferior form reactive and overwhelming

concepts.title: Sensation Function: Concrete Fact Perception
concepts.description: discriminates present/absent; perceives concrete reality; external (extraverted sensation: facts engaged externally) or internal (introverted sensation: subtle body sensation); superior form grounds in practical reality; inferior form overwhelmed by sensation or disconnected from it

concepts.title: Intuition Function: Pattern and Possibility Perception
concepts.description: discriminates becoming/stable; perceives pattern and implication; external (extraverted intuition: possibilities perceived externally) or internal (introverted intuition: archetypal meaning perceived internally); superior form visionary; inferior form paranoid or obsessive

concepts.title: Transcendent Function: The Only Mechanism for Integration
concepts.description: the psyche's ability to hold contradictions without collapse; operates through symbol, not logic; generates meaning by containing paradox; the function through which the Self becomes conscious; accessible only when the superior function releases its grip

concepts.title: Symbol as Living Form: Distinction from Metaphor and Allegory
concepts.description: contains paradox without resolution; alive because it grows with the viewer; regenerative; generates meaning through the tension it holds; difference from metaphor (dead comparison) and allegory (represents something else); the language of the unconscious and of spirit

concepts.title: Inferior Function: Neurotic Gateway and Path to Wholeness
concepts.description: opposite to superior; undifferentiated; primitive; source of neurosis through possession; yet the gateway to the unconscious and the Self; development is impossible; integration requires conscious relationship (not elimination or perfection); eruption signals compensation at work

concepts.title: Anima/Animus: The Inner Feminine and Masculine
concepts.description: the contrasexual dimension of the psyche; not opposite sex but the opposite-coded functions within you; shaped by biology, culture, parental imago; operates through projection in relationships; integration requires withdrawing projections and meeting the inner figure

concepts.title: Libido: Psychic Energy as Universal Life Force
concepts.description: not sexual drive but psychic energy itself; flows into whatever commands attention; concentrates, depletes, regenerates; superior function is primary channel; lifespan redistribution (first half: ego-building; second half: meaning-making); blocked libido produces neurotic symptoms

concepts.title: Consciousness vs. Unconscious: The Divided Psyche
concepts.description: consciousness is thin film on vast ocean; unconscious autonomous and often opposed to conscious intention; personal unconscious (recoverable) vs. collective unconscious (inherited); dialogue through dream, symbol, active imagination

concepts.title: Differentiation vs. Undifferentiation: The Work of Becoming Conscious
concepts.description: refinement and separation of functions so each operates with clarity; undifferentiated functions blurry and reactive; integration requires more than differentiation—requires conscious relationship with the opposite

concepts.title: Neurotic Patterns by Type: The Signature Neuroses
concepts.description: each type has characteristic neurotic shape; thinking-type neurosis: possession by logic, starvation of feeling; feeling-type: possession by values, alienation from logic; sensation-type: possession by concrete, starvation of meaning; intuitive-type: possession by possibility, disconnection from reality; inferior function eruptions are compensation

concepts.title: Jung and Vedic Psychology: Parallel Structures of Self and Consciousness
concepts.description: Jung's Self functionally identical to Vedic Atman (ultimate self beyond ego); prana = libido; three bodies (waking/dream/deep sleep) parallel type theory; samadhi = active imagination; integration in both systems requires moving beyond ego-consciousness

concepts.title: Jung and Taoism: The Psychology of Wu Wei and Complementary Opposites

concepts.description: Construct-Aware (web-weaving, language as technology, grand theory trap, ego transcendence paradox, spiritual ego trap, self-deception jiu-jitsu, existential aloneness, ~1.5%); Unitive (direct consciousness, witnessing, peaks-become-ground, controlled folly, dissolution of evil, ~0.5%); Layer A vs Layer B distinction throughout

concepts.title: Mystical Experience Stage Interpretation
concepts.description: stage as interpretive container for genuine mystical experience; same experience pours into different containers by stage; Conformist (exclusive truth claim), Expert/Achiever (neurological anomaly), Pluralist (all paths valid), Strategist (developmental mission), Construct-Aware (spiritual ego trap), Unitive (container dissolves into content)

title: The Inner Critic and Consciousness Development (Stone, Hal & Sidra Stone — Embracing Your Inner Critic, 1993)

concepts.title: Inner Critic (Core)
concepts.description: the universal protector voice that took over consciousness; development from genuine protection to autonomy and hypervigilance; primary mechanism preventing vulnerability; three-layer structure (attack on self / judgment / absolute authority); why the Critic cannot be eliminated but must be transformed; integrated framework showing how Critic operates across all subpersonality clusters

concepts.title: The Incomparable Comparer
concepts.description: the comparison mechanism that maintains shame; comparing your beginning to someone else's middle; the Critic's most effective creative block mechanism; measurement against imagined standards; three mechanisms of comparison (timeline confusion, skill-level confusion, completeness confusion)

concepts.title: Relational Anxiety — "What Will People Think?"
concepts.description: the core anxiety driving the Critic's operation; imagined judgment as more paralyzing than real rejection; the Invisible Audience in the head; differentiation between reputation-management (rational) and relational-anxiety (terror-based); how relational anxiety generates the Critic's absolute authority

concepts.title: Voice Dialogue Framework
concepts.description: structured methodology for separating consciousness from identification with subpersonalities through dialogue; not merging with the Critic but developing conscious relationship; the Aware Ego position as observing center; how dialogue produces visible separation through writing

concepts.title: Operating Ego vs. Aware Ego
concepts.description: identification with a single primary self (Operating Ego) versus consciousness that can observe all selves (Aware Ego); development process; how Aware Ego emerges from Voice Dialogue; the freedom that comes from witness position rather than identification

concepts.title: Journal Writing and Voice Dialogue as Method
concepts.description: self-directed written dialogue with subpersonalities; simple protocol (choose part, ask question, let it answer); what becomes visible through writing; how regular practice develops separation and Aware Ego; limitations and deepenings compared to facilitated dialogue

concepts.title: Subpersonality Pairs (Primary & Disowned Selves)
concepts.description: every primary self has equal opposite disowned; Pleaser disowns Anger; Independent disowns Neediness; the energy cost of suppression; why the disowned self becomes neurotic; four channels disowned selves use to communicate

concepts.title: Vulnerable Child / Inner Child
concepts.description: the exiled authentic self beneath all protective personas; characteristics of the original self; why it must be protected; Critic's job as guarding this child's fear; integration pathway through Aware Ego protection

concepts.title: Disowned Self Projection & Judgment
concepts.description: harsh judgment of others reveals disowned selves; what we judge reveals what we suppress; mechanism of projection; why judgment is diagnostic; path from judgment to integration

concepts.title: The Rule Maker
concepts.description: the subpersonality that establishes shoulds and rules about how to be; universal structure underlying the Perfectionist, Achiever, and Moralist; separation from the rules vs. identification with them; legitimate function when not primary

concepts.title: Personal vs. Impersonal Energy
concepts.description: hooked into others' reactions versus task-oriented; personal energy = reactive to judgment; impersonal energy = focused on the work itself; how shift from personal to impersonal energy liberates creativity and presence

concepts.title: Bonding Patterns and Regressive Dynamics
concepts.description: how complementary subpersonalities in different people lock together without awareness; the good mother/helpless child bonding as structural pair; self-sustaining dynamics; why awareness of the pattern begins to disrupt it

concepts.title: Inner Child Abuse Cycle: The Critic's Attack on the Self
concepts.description: perpetual dynamic of Critic attacking Vulnerable Child; child's terror triggering more attack (feedback loop); how the cycle becomes self-sustaining; the paradox that attack meant to protect deepens what it protects against; what breaks the cycle

concepts.title: Shame, Depression, and Low Self-Esteem as Critic's Outcomes
concepts.description: interconnected psychological states all driven by Critic's operation; shame as the Critic's primary weapon; depression as learned helplessness from endless attack; low self-esteem as internalized voice; how these three reinforce each other

concepts.title: Silencing & The Critic's Inability to Tolerate Silence
concepts.description: Critic's need to fill silence with anxiety; noise-making to prevent internal stillness; meditation difficulty for strong Critic; what Critic fears in silence; how quieting creates space for Vulnerable Child's voice

concepts.title: Underlying Anxiety & Conversion Principle
concepts.description: terror beneath the Critic's attacks (not actual judgment but protection from catastrophe); addressing the underlying terror reduces Critic's necessity to attack; how fear converts into attack as survival mechanism; what transformation requires

concepts.title: The Objective Mind: The Critic Transformed Into Creative Ally
concepts.description: transformation of the Critic's energy into clear seeing without cruelty; discernment, quality consciousness, authentic authority; difference from the attacking Critic (fear-based vs. clarity-based); how transformation occurs; what becomes possible

concepts.title: Inner Patriarch and Gender Differences
concepts.description: gendered manifestations of the Critic; five thousand years of patriarchal internalization in women; strength/competition/success demands in men; domain-specific Critic operations (body, sexuality, anger, ambition, motherhood for women; power, emotion, vulnerability for men); cultural consciousness required for liberation

title: Trauma and Soul Integration (Kalsched, Donald — Trauma and the Soul, 2013)

concepts.title: Bilateral Brain Integration in Trauma Recovery
concepts.description: right/left hemisphere dissociation mechanism; five stages (activation without flooding → somatic markers → wordless-to-language translation); clinical recognition; integration as prerequisite for soul contact

concepts.title: Embodied Presence and Somatic Unconscious
concepts.description: body as archive of trauma knowledge; somatic unconscious (procedural memory + affective tone + physiological responses + transgenerational memory); four-step recovery pathway toward embodied presence

concepts.title: Inner Objects and Their Autonomy
concepts.description: semi-autonomous internalized presences from caregivers; formation under trauma pressure; persecutory/idealized/seductive/abandoning object types; working relationally rather than rationally with inner figures

concepts.title: Psychic Retreat and Anti-Wholeness Defenses
concepts.description: defended space against healing; protective system's paradox (protection becomes prison); sealed chamber with sentries and diversions; clinical recognition of apparent-treatability collapse

concepts.title: Innocent Suffering vs. Neurotic Suffering
concepts.description: innocent (genuine loss that teaches) vs. neurotic (repetitive self-created loop); neurotic as recognizable pattern; gateway of recognizing the difference

concepts.title: Descent and Harrowing: The Journey into the Underworld
concepts.description: descent as necessary for genuine healing; Dante geography mapping; harrowing as return with imprisoned soul-child; managed regression with witness presence

concepts.title: The Child Archetype in World Mythology
concepts.description: divine child threatened across mythology (Chiron, Krishna, Horus, Christ); guardian as jailer; mythological persistence; soul-child as the imprisoned authentic self

concepts.title: Synchronicity and Archetypal Timing (Kairos)
concepts.description: meaningful coincidence as Self's communication; chronos vs. kairos distinction; protective system disrupting synchronicity; three forms of synchronicity in healing work

concepts.title: The Inner World as Sanctuary or Prison
concepts.description: inner world quality determined by internalized objects; internalized good parent vs. bad objects; reclaiming embodied presence in inner world; transformation pathway

concepts.title: Shadow Integration and Trauma
concepts.description: shadow as disowned authentic self; trauma's creation of monstrous shadow; unintegrated shadow as genuinely dangerous; four-step integration path

concepts.title: Shame and Identity: How "I Am Bad" Becomes the Organizing Principle
concepts.description: shame as "I am wrong" (not "I did wrong"); internalized voice of perpetrator; distinction from genuine conscience; recovery pathway through recognition

title: Behavioral Economics and Decision Science (Thaler, Misbehaving, 2015)

concepts.title: Homo Economicus vs. Homo Sapiens
concepts.description: The core tension: economic theory's rational-actor fiction vs. how humans actually decide; why the Econ model persists despite empirical failure; systematic ways humans diverge

concepts.title: Loss Aversion: Asymmetric Valuation
concepts.description: Foundational mechanism: losses hurt ~2x more than equivalent gains feel good; value function shape (concave for gains, convex for losses); evolutionary logic; applications across decision domains

concepts.title: Endowment Effect: Ownership Premium
concepts.description: Ownership inflates valuation 50-100%; WTA > WTP for identical items; four mechanisms: loss aversion activation, entitlement anchoring, identity blending, imaginative elaboration

concepts.title: Reference Dependence and Anchors
concepts.description: All value is reference-dependent; same outcome feels different by reference point; four mechanisms: status quo as default, expectations/entitlements, arbitrary anchoring, social comparison

concepts.title: Supposedly Irrelevant Factors (SIFs)
concepts.description: Factors Econ theory says irrelevant (sunk costs, fairness, transaction utility) but empirically matter; not irrational but reflect adaptive psychological features; examples: concert tickets, wage cuts, reference prices

concepts.title: Mental Accounting Framework
concepts.description: How people psychologically organize money into categories with different rules and loss thresholds; four dimensions: source, purpose, time horizon, loss threshold; creates artificial constraints despite nominal wealth

concepts.title: Transaction Utility: Bargains and Ripoffs
concepts.description: Pleasure/pain of deal independent of product quality; reference price determines transaction utility; fairness perception key; explains bundling, price transparency effects, negotiated pricing satisfaction

concepts.title: Sunk Cost Fallacy: Mechanisms and Entitlement
concepts.description: Why people continue bad investments/situations; three mechanisms: loss aversion, entitlement/obligation, identity/commitment signals; distinction between fallacy and legitimate commitment

concepts.title: Mental Budgets and Segregation
concepts.description: Money segregated into separate mental accounts creates artificial constraints; cash poverty despite nominal wealth; "pay yourself first" works through segregation not willpower

concepts.title: House Money Effect: Windfall Spending
concepts.description: Windfall money activates different spending rules than earned income; feels "free" to spend; weaker loss aversion; explains spending paradox

concepts.title: Nominal Illusion: Wage Frames
concepts.description: Same real outcome feels different by nominal frame (wage cut rejected; wage freeze accepted); determines fairness perception despite identical real outcome

concepts.title: Reference Price as Entitlement
concepts.description: Established price becomes psychological right; increases feel like violations; explains price stickiness, customer anger, fairness more than supply-demand

concepts.title: Planner vs. Doer: Dual Selves in Conflict
concepts.description: Two competing decision systems: cool Planner vs. hot impulsive Doer; time-inconsistent preferences; temporal discounting creates gap between intentions and execution

concepts.title: Present Bias and Hyperbolic Discounting
concepts.description: Steep discount rate for immediate future, equal for distant futures (hyperbolic, not exponential); generates procrastination, addiction, diet failures; sophisticated vs. naive discounters

concepts.title: Self-Control and Commitment Devices
concepts.description: Self-control is environmental design not willpower; commitment devices (autopay, accountability, penalties, habits) bind Doer to Planner's plans without willpower; removing choice more effective than increasing willpower

concepts.title: Fairness Norms in Pricing
concepts.description: Prices constrained by fairness norms more than supply-demand; cost visibility, profit margins, universal application determine fairness; firms don't maximize short-term profit if it violates fairness

concepts.title: Price Gouging and Emergency Pricing
concepts.description: Extreme fairness violation: exploiting desperation triggers moral outrage; firms choosing fairness-optimal over profit-optimal pricing because fairness violations destroy reputation

concepts.title: Preference Reversals: Selling vs. Buying
concepts.description: WTA > WTP for identical items; loss aversion + endowment effect create asymmetry; explains real estate, wage, insurance market gaps

concepts.title: Perceived Fairness and Preference Reversals
concepts.description: Frame (buying vs. selling) activates different fairness standards; same price fair in buying frame, unfair in selling frame; fairness frame-dependent not objective

concepts.title: Firm Reputation vs. Short-Term Profit
concepts.description: Profitable firms don't maximize short-term price; fairness violations destroy reputation worth more than gained profit; long-term profit maximization requires fairness constraints

concepts.title: Market Entry and Fair Dealing
concepts.description: New competitors face asymmetric fairness constraints: incumbents can violate fairness (reputation buffer); newcomers cannot; creates reputation equilibrium lock preventing entry

concepts.title: Narrow Framing: Daily Targets and Risk Aversion
concepts.description: Evaluating decisions in isolation produces excessive risk aversion; single coin flip feels risky; ten flips feel acceptable (identical EV); explains excessive trading, risk aversion, unrealistic targets

concepts.title: Myopic Loss Aversion: Portfolio Checking Frequency
concepts.description: Checking frequency determines behavior; daily feels too risky; annual feels acceptable (identical risk); explains equity premium puzzle (6% gap); solution: change evaluation horizon

concepts.title: Overconfidence in Markets
concepts.description: Traders overestimate skill despite incentives for accuracy; persists through attribution bias, weak feedback, recency bias; leads to excessive trading, concentrated bets, risk underestimation

concepts.title: Equity Premium Puzzle
concepts.description: Stocks return 6% more than bonds; theory predicts 0.35% gap; gap explained by myopic loss aversion (checking frequency makes volatility feel excessive); solution: reduce checking

concepts.title: Stock Market Bubbles and Overvaluation
concepts.description: Bubbles driven by overconfidence, herding, narrative capture, loss aversion; five-phase lifecycle: foundation→takeoff→euphoria→peak→collapse; rational investors cannot reliably pop them

concepts.title: Availability Heuristic and Fear
concepts.description: Risk perception based on vividness not statistics; plane crashes feel riskier than driving; drives insurance purchasing, financial overreactions, systematic risk misperception

concepts.title: Anchoring and Adjustment
concepts.description: First number offered disproportionately influences final estimate; adjustment insufficient; biased toward anchor; critical in negotiations: first offer determines outcome more than fundamentals

concepts.title: Efficient Market Hypothesis and Its Limits
concepts.description: EMH assumes rationality; evidence shows semi-efficiency; anomalies persist (equity premium, bubbles, professional traders, seasonal patterns); markets incorporate info well long-term, short-term distortions

title: Shame and Concealment

concepts.title: Shame as Survival System
concepts.description: shame as evolved tribal-exclusion-avoidance mechanism; formative event → Never Again rule → concealment strategy → personality; three exceptions; long-term costs; cosmological anchor (Vedic pāśas cross-domain)

concepts.title: Concealment Archetypes
concepts.description: seven behavioral configurations around protected fears: Controller, Performer, Achiever, Moralist, Helper, Dominator, Withdrawer; characteristic cost each cannot avoid; Enneagram parallel flagged; approval-seeking pathways genesis stories

concepts.title: Insult as Identity Marker
concepts.description: insult as boundary marker (we judge what we cannot afford to be); four-step shadow diagnostic; four judgment categories; empathy as accurate vision without contempt

title: Prenatal Origins and Developmental Psychology

concepts.title: Prenatal Consciousness and Sentience
concepts.description: wanted/unwanted babies longitudinal study (WHO/NIH/Ford Foundation, 3 countries, 25 years); twelve prenatal senses (first 10 defensible); habituation learning studies (Sandman, Hepper, DeCasper); Sophie's Choice indirect maternal-fetal transmission; consciousness-before-brain claim [LOW CONFIDENCE]; cross-domain: shame-internalization-mechanisms Layer 0 + soul-cosmology-hub pre-embodiment gap

title: Survival Strategies and Consciousness

concepts.title: Conscious vs. Opportunistic Survival
concepts.description: agency in defense mechanisms; unconscious/opportunistic survival (reactive, defensive, identity-fused); conscious survival (sees the pattern, chooses deliberately); developmental stage-gating (Expert and above); conscious choice model

concepts.title: Armor, Upgrading, and Identity Dissolution
concepts.description: identity fused with armor; upgrading requires grieving the loss of the approval source; phases: recognition → grief → seeking new function → identity reformation; what replaces old defense

concepts.title: Approval-Seeking Pathways
concepts.description: how each concealment archetype formed through specific approval sources; Performer (approval for entertainment), Achiever (for accomplishment), Moralist (for virtue), Helper (for usefulness), Controller (for order), Dominator (for strength), Withdrawer (for invisibility); persistence mechanism; when approval sources vanish

concepts.title: Love and Suffering as Dual Shapers
concepts.description: same relationship provides both wounding and compensation; parent as both source of shame and (failed) solution-provider; dual shaping mechanism; why integration is complex (losing wound means losing compensation structure)

concepts.title: Inadequacy as Constructed Reality
concepts.description: inadequacy as invented through comparison; Achiever/Expert-stage depression as attainment trap instantiation; Rest as Danger mechanism; constructed-ness opens possibility of reconstruction

concepts.title: Development vs. Spiritual Transcendence
concepts.description: vertical development (building new capacities) vs. horizontal transcendence (accessing what's already present); vertical development necessary but insufficient for spiritual work; post-conventional stages and spiritual ego traps; transcendence-not-development thesis

concepts.title: Epistemology of Survival
concepts.description: defense mechanisms as cognitive gatekeepers; denial-rationalization-ideology cascade; how survival patterns shape what you can *know* about reality; defense prevents consciousness of the defense; defense prevents knowing the original wound exists

title: Attention and Mind Governance

concepts.title: Jinshin/Doshin — The Dual Mind
concepts.description: doshin (heaven-aligned, detached from motive) vs. jinshin (reactive, self-interested); nine-tradition convergence (six continents, ten centuries); courage of duty; still-mirror principle; daily polishing instruction; Adachi's physical/basic mind added as ninth tradition (somatic/developmental)

concepts.title: Demonic Attitudes Catalogue — Suzuki Shosan
concepts.description: 17 nameable depressive/destructive attitudes that drain warrior vitality (negligent through oblivious-of-death); seven feelings as illness sources (joy, anger, sorrow, care, pity, fear, surprise); negligence as the enabling condition for all others; 17th-century Japanese Buddhist-warrior parallel to Western concealment archetypes; Suzuki Shosan (1579–1655)

concepts.title: Metsuke and Perceptual Attention
concepts.description: Yagyū Munenori's perceptual discipline; six principles including Enzan no Metsuke (soft gaze/peripheral awareness), kan (discernment without paralysis), still water equanimity, reading intention; two primary Japanese martial arts sources

title: Integral Theory (Cross-Domain)

concepts.title: Associative Purity
concepts.description: cognitive failure mode native to Level Six (Utopian) processing; ideological paralysis caused by inability to act without perfect moral alignment; coined by Hartwell for Integral Storytelling framework; **primary home: creative-practice/ (Integral Storytelling Hub)**

title: Influence and Persuasion

concepts.title: PCP Model (Perception → Context → Permission)
concepts.description: three-stage behavioral influence framework; language as resonance; script surfacing; SCOP defense (Focus/Authority/Tribe/Emotion); SCOP self-defeat admission

concepts.title: Fractionation and Suggestability
concepts.description: fractionation as emotional state cycling → hyper-suggestibility; suggestability as context-dependent state; six modifiable factors; applied to mass media and algorithmic feeds

concepts.title: Writing as Applied Psychology
concepts.description: writing as engineering of reader's experience; reader-first orientation; atomic units of agreement; frame shift; reach from ground truth; cognitive hospitality; self-generated idea effect (two-Lego technique)

title: Power, Authority, and Social Navigation

concepts.title: Grandiosity
concepts.description: four-type taxonomy (Legend, Inflated Mediocrity, Latent, Deflated); four behavioral warning signs; four-step calibration protocol; Strategist self-inflation trap; Construct-Aware spiritual ego trap (self-transparency as building material for the most sophisticated grandiosity)

concepts.title: Social Force and Conformity
concepts.description: social force as the invisible current of group norms operating below conscious awareness; seven courtier types (Perfect Courtier, Sycophant, Critic, Enforcer, Contrarian, Intriguer, Climber); personal credo as counter-protocol; Asch/Milgram empirical backbone

concepts.title: Fickleness and Authority
concepts.description: structural ambivalence in every authority relationship (desire + resentment coexisting permanently); three fickleness triggers (visible uncertainty, favoritism, individual invisibility); authority formula: confidence + consistency + recognition; assertion paradox

concepts.title: Anima/Animus Projection
concepts.description: unconscious contralateral side (Anima in men, Animus in women) projected onto others; four stages of development (Eve/Helen/Mary/Sophia; physical power/romantic hero/word/meaning-maker); projection sequence; negative possession; four-step integration path

concepts.title: Mortality Awareness
concepts.description: Becker's Denial of Death: most behavior driven by unconscious mortality terror; four immortality projects (biological, creative, spiritual, social/political); Sublime as ego-dissolution rehearsal; deliberate death-meditation; conscious project choice as counter-protocol; TMT empirical grounding; Hoffer's frustrated-self as convergent mechanism: both self-contempt and mortality terror produce flight from individual selfhood (Cross-Domain Handshake added)

concepts.title: Shadow Integration
concepts.description: the sealed room: qualities suppressed by early social environment, not destroyed but partitioned; projection diagnostic; four integration steps; Pluralist stage as when sub-personalities surface; self-immunity illusion as shadow dynamic

concepts.title: Narcissism Spectrum
concepts.description: four types (Complete Narcissist, Narcissistic Wound, Functional Narcissist, Healthy Narcissist); healthy vs. narcissistic self-regard are structurally different, not merely degrees; four empathic navigation skills; strategic mirroring; invisible structural boundaries

concepts.title: Envy Dynamics
concepts.description: five envier types (Overtly Hostile, Passive Aggressive, Praise-and-Undermine, Self-Deprecating, Status Competitor); Mitfreude (Nietzsche) as counter-force; navigation strategies; envy as diagnostic pointing toward authentic want

concepts.title: Life Purpose Framework
concepts.description: Primal Force as organizing energy beneath all false purposes; four false purposes; Pluralist turning inward as first moment Life's Task is genuinely askable; Strategist transformational mission as its post-conventional expression

concepts.title: Compulsive Behavior
concepts.description: rational irrationality: compulsive patterns as solutions to formative problems, persisting beyond original context; four-step identification protocol (identify pattern → trigger → formative origin → counter-move)

concepts.title: Covetousness and Desire
concepts.description: grass-is-greener structure: possession domesticates, desire concentrates on unavailable; desire contagion (spreads socially before attaching to object); three strategies (scarcity, social proof, strategic absence)

title: Mass Psychology and Public Opinion (Bernays / *The Edward Bernays Reader*, 2021 + *Crystallizing Public Opinion*, 1923)

concepts.title: Group Psychology and Herd Instinct Doctrine
concepts.description: Trotter's logic-proof compartments; group leader multiplier; Martin's crowd-as-state-of-mind (isolated reader = crowd member through temporal coordination); a priori judgment; Prohibition repeal / velvet cascade / NAACP Atlanta evidence; Hoffer's inversion: self-escape as alternative to herd-orientation mechanism (Tension 4 added)

concepts.title: Potential Converts Taxonomy
concepts.description: 11 types with distinct conversion pathways; new poor (most volatile — imagination from prior condition + grievance from loss); abjectly poor (not primary recruits — survival absorbs all surplus energy); sinners (guilt redirected outward as hatred); bored (drama and historical participation as drivers); De Tocqueville rising-conditions paradox embedded

concepts.title: Stereotype and A Priori Judgment
concepts.description: Lippmann's "pictures in our heads"; five-step sequence; three-mode engagement (using / combating / creating stereotypes); Roosevelt's "square deal" as stereotype creation; decay problem; PR tactical implications

concepts.title: Public Opinion as Interaction
concepts.description: opinion as river (product of upstream forces) not lake (fixed body); five interacting forces; malleable vs. identity-attached belief distinction; plural publics model; measurement-vs-manufacturing paradox in polling

title: Charisma and Acquired Presence (Moynahan / *Rasputin: The Saint Who Sinned*, 1997)

concepts.title: Charismatic Gaze as Acquired Craft
concepts.description: five independent witness categories (hostile / devoted / ambivalent / professional / bureaucratic) all describe identical attentional quality; Makari of Verkhoturye and hesychast practice as origin; Rapid Janus Flip; contemplative practice as charisma precondition (not charisma techniques); genuine-presence enabling-exploitation paradox; the authentic practice makes the exploit possible

title: Manipulation and Influence — Psychological Mechanisms (Coxall / *Human Manipulation: A Handbook*)

concepts.title: Authority Costume
concepts.description: Appearance of competence through signaling; authority bias exploitation

concepts.title: Flattery and Charm
concepts.description: False rapport building; bonding mechanism hijacking

concepts.title: Isolation and Separation
concepts.description: Control through disconnection; outside perspective removal

concepts.title: Timing Games
concepts.description: Planning fallacy and deadline pressure exploitation

concepts.title: Symbolic Reward
concepts.description: Status without substance; prestige substituting for pay

concepts.title: Emotional Manipulation
concepts.description: Weaponizing vulnerability lists (Braiker, Simon, Kantor); persistence and accessibility

concepts.title: Self-Delusion Mechanisms
concepts.description: 21 sub-types (learned helplessness, backfire effect, anchoring, loss aversion, etc.)

concepts.title: Fear Exploitation
concepts.description: Existential, social, and phobic fear manufacturing; fear-conditioning resistance to logic

title: Political Violence and Radicalization (Boot / *Invisible Armies*, 2013)

concepts.title: Terrorist Mind — Motivation and Psychology
concepts.description: psychologically ordinary people; four-component motivational architecture (grievance, identity/belonging, significance quest, moral licensing); radicalization as social process; what doesn't predict terrorism (poverty alone, mental illness, religion alone); deradicalization failure modes

concepts.title: Recruitment and Radicalization Mechanics
concepts.description: radicalization as social process not ideological conversion; pre-modern vs. modern recruitment; friendship-based recruitment; gradual commitment escalation; social isolation as mechanism; ISIS social media as self-radicalization infrastructure; counter-radicalization failure modes

concepts.title: Nationalism vs. Ideology — Durability of Commitment
concepts.description: nationalist claims sustain insurgent commitment longer than ideological claims; four psychological mechanisms (identity fusion, legibility, self-evident justice, external resonance); historical evidence (most vs. least durable insurgencies); the foreign-government trap; counterinsurgent legitimacy problem

title: Somatic Trauma Theory (Levine 1997 + Scaer 2001)

concepts.title: Somatic Trauma Theory
concepts.description: Levine's core framework: trauma as incomplete physiological response; four-component sequence (hyperarousal → constriction → dissociation → helplessness/freeze); self-perpetuating arousal cycle; dis-ease-not-disease model; positive integration vision; **Scaer added**: MRI hippocampal reduction + PET activation corroboration; Kardiner 1941 physioneurosis; Schore maternal-infant right OFC development

concepts.title: Freeze Response and Immobility
concepts.description: tonic immobility as third survival option; involuntary biological braking; trembling/shaking discharge; animal resolution humans interrupt; **Scaer added**: polyvagal architecture (DVC freeze vs. VVC social engagement — not the same parasympathetic system); IS (immobility stress) model: restraint itself traumatogenic

concepts.title: Renegotiation vs. Re-enactment
concepts.description: titrated oscillation between trauma vortex and healing vortex; triumph signal as somatic completion marker; **Scaer added**: pharmacological layer — opioid cycling makes reenactment compulsive not merely persistent; spousal abuse cycle as endorphin loop

concepts.title: Felt Sense and Somatic Awareness
concepts.description: Gendlin's pre-verbal gestalt body-intelligence; tracking as primary therapeutic vehicle; **Scaer added**: two memory systems diverge under high arousal (nondeclarative enhanced, declarative impaired); alexithymia as named blockage at felt sense-to-language bridge

concepts.title: Dissociation and Cognitive Freeze
concepts.description: consciousness withdrawing from unbearable experience; full Livingstone quote; spectrum from highway hypnosis to multiple personality; four dissociative split types; **Scaer added**: somatic dissociation as body-level complement (measurable via thermography/blood flow)

concepts.title: Societal and Cultural Trauma
concepts.description: collective trauma as amplification of individual mechanism; ethological baseline; Eskimo singing duels; mass re-enactment hypothesis; cultural rituals as lost discharge practices

concepts.title: Delayed Traumatic Reactions
concepts.description: latency period = suppression not healing; trigger = somatic signature match not narrative match; Johnny bike case; medical procedures as most common antecedent; first-hours prevention window; invisible cumulative accumulation

concepts.title: The Brain Architecture of Trauma
concepts.description: thalamic gateway and olfactory bypass (direct amygdala access — explains MCS); HPA axis vs. SAM axis; HPA paradox (acute PTSD = high cortisol; chronic PTSD = LOW cortisol → immune brake removed → autoimmune risk); Mason's revision of Selye (psychological meaning, not stimulus intensity, activates the cascade); endorphin system; anterior cingulate as extinction-learning circuit suppressed by chronic PTSD

concepts.title: Kindling and Trauma Perpetuation
concepts.description: subthreshold stimuli summate → self-sustaining amygdala circuit; the delay between trauma and symptom onset explained; one-trial learning (life-threatening events encoded permanently in procedural memory — never extinguished, only suppressed); right OFC impairment from prior trauma makes each subsequent trauma easier to kindle

concepts.title: Somatic Dissociation
concepts.description: regional autonomic dysregulation in body areas most involved in the original threat; measurable (thermography, blood flow, hair growth); piriformis syndrome 31/31 [UNVERIFIED — needs primary source]; speech disorders as somatic dissociation at Broca's area; spasmodic torticollis as frozen orienting reflex; RSD self-case

concepts.title: Diseases of Traumatic Stress
concepts.description: fibromyalgia, CFS, IBS, RSD, interstitial cystitis, migraine, autoimmune as kindling diseases; cortisol paradox (low chronic cortisol removes immune brake → autoimmune risk); Kardiner's physioneurosis (1941) as 60-year antecedent; DESNOS as better clinical predictor than DSM-IV PTSD

concepts.title: Trauma Reenactment and the Endorphin Mechanism
concepts.description: infant-caregiver opioid template (separation → endorphin drop; reunion → endorphin release) drives adult reenactment; Perry's gender model (internalizing vs. externalizing patterns); anniversary syndrome; narcotic antagonist dilemma

concepts.title: Alexithymia and Speechless Terror
concepts.description: no words for feelings; Broca's area goes offline during high amygdala activation (PET-documented); speechless terror; structural ceiling on verbal therapy; speech disorders as somatic dissociation; what EMDR does that verbal therapy cannot

concepts.title: Autonomic Oscillation
concepts.description: Antelman: healthy systems cycle through full activation/rest extremes; ergotropic and trophotropic poles as complementary not opposed; the stuck oscillation (neither full activation nor full rest) as the chronic trauma state; pranayama as oscillation rehabilitation

concepts.title: Character as Procedural Learning
concepts.description: Grigsby/Hartlaub: character is largely procedural memory; trauma as character formation; conservation withdrawal (Schore) as prototype; two memory systems (declarative vs. procedural — different systems, cannot cross-update); ceiling on insight-based character change

concepts.title: Sources of Trauma: A Taxonomy
